results.title: Drag-Along Right
results._source_text_exact: 3.1 If holders of a majority of the outstanding Preferred Stock and a majority of the board approve a sale of Contracting Party A, all other shareholders shall be required to vote in favor of and consent to such transaction ("Drag-Along Right"), subject to customary protections regarding consideration and representations.
results.original_text: 3.1 If holders of a majority of the outstanding Preferred Stock and a majority of the board approve a sale of Contracting Party A, all other shareholders shall be required to vote in favor of and consent to such transaction ("Drag-Along Right"), subject to customary protections regarding consideration and representations.
results.reasoning_evidence: 3.1 If holders of a majority of the outstanding Preferred Stock and a majority of the board approve a sale of Contracting Party A, all other shareholders shall be requi
results.quoted_text: 3.1 If holders of a majority of the outstanding Preferred Stock and a majority of the board approve a sale of Contracting Party A, all other shareholders shall be required to vote in favor of and consent to such transaction ("Drag-Along Right"), subject to customary protections regarding consideration and r
results.explanation_simple: This clause mandates that minority shareholders must agree to a sale if a majority approves it.
results.why_it_matters: It can limit the ability of minority shareholders to oppose a sale, potentially impacting their financial interests.
results.legal_insight: This clause affects control, approvals, compliance duties, audit rights, assignment, or governance processes.
results.market_comparison: This clause is common in investment agreements to facilitate sales but may be seen as favoring majority shareholders.
results.red_flag_reason: The clause imposes significant restrictions on minority shareholders' rights, which could lead to potential conflicts.
results.recommendation: Check approval rights, assignment limits, audit scope, compliance obligations, subcontracting controls, and consequences of breach.
results.safer_alternative: Include provisions that ensure minority shareholders receive fair consideration and adequate representation during the sale process.
results.negotiation_advice: Consider requiring prior written consent for assignment, transfer or delegation, while allowing reasonable exceptions for affiliates, group restructuring, mergers, acquisitions, or sale of substantially all assets where the assignee can perform the obligations.
section_path: 3. DRAG-ALONG AND TAG-ALONG RIGHTS

3.1 If holders of a majority of the outstanding Preferred Stock and a majority of the board approve a sale of Contracting Party A, all other shareholders shall be required to vote in favor of and consent to such transaction ("Drag-Along Right"), subject to customary protections regarding consideration and representations.

results.title: Tag-Along Right
results._source_text_exact: 3.2 If a founder proposes to transfer shares representing more than 5% of Contracting Party A's outstanding capital stock, the Investor shall have the right to participate in such sale on the same terms ("Tag-Along Right").

results.explanation_simple: The clause grants the Investor a right to participate in share sales exceeding 5%.
results.why_it_matters: This right protects the Investor's interests by allowing them to sell shares under the same conditions as the founder, potentially preventing dilution of their investment.

results.title: Shareholder Transfer Restrictions
results._source_text_exact: 5.1 No shareholder shall transfer any shares of Contracting Party A's capital stock except in compliance with this Agreement, including a right of first refusal in favor of Contracting Party A and then the other shareholders, and compliance with applicable securities laws.
results.original_text: 5.1 No shareholder shall transfer any shares of Contracting Party A's capital stock except in compliance with this Agreement, including a right of first refusal in favor of Contracting Party A and then the other shareholders, and compliance with applicable securities laws.

results.explanation_simple: This clause restricts the transfer of shares by shareholders.
results.why_it_matters: It ensures that any transfer of shares is controlled and complies with the agreement and legal requirements, which can affect shareholder liquidity and control.
results.legal_insight: This clause affects control, approvals, compliance duties, audit rights, assignment, or governance processes.
results.market_comparison: This type of clause is common in shareholder agreements to maintain control within the existing shareholder group.
results.recommendation: Check approval rights, assignment limits, audit scope, compliance obligations, subcontracting controls, and consequences of breach.
results.negotiation_advice: Consider requiring prior written consent for assignment, transfer or delegation, while allowing reasonable exceptions for affiliates, group restructuring, mergers, acquisitions, or sale of substantially all assets where the assignee can perform the obligations.

results.title: Board of Directors Composition
results._source_text_exact: 1.1 Contracting Party A's board of directors shall consist of five (5) members: two (2) designated by the founders, one (1) designated by the Investor for so long as the Investor holds at least 5% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, and two (2) independent directors mutually agreed upon by the founders and the Investor.
results.original_text: 1.1 Contracting Party A's board of directors shall consist of five (5) members: two (2) designated by the founders, one (1) designated by the Investor for so long as the Investor holds at least 5% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, and two (2) independent directors mutually agreed upon by the founders and the Investor.
results.reasoning_evidence: 1.1 Contracting Party A's board of directors shall consist of five (5) members: two (2) designated by the founders, one (1) designated by the Investor for so long as th
results.quoted_text: Contracting Party A's board of directors shall consist of five (5) members: two (2) designated by the founders, one (1) designated by the Investor for so long as the Investor holds at least 5% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, and two (2) independent directors mutually agreed upon by the founders and the Investor.
results.explanation_simple: The clause specifies the composition of the board of directors, including designated members by founders and the Investor.
results.why_it_matters: The composition of the board affects decision-making power and control within Contracting Party A, particularly the influence of the Investor.
results.market_comparison: This structure is somewhat common in startup governance, where founders retain significant control while allowing for investor representation.
results.recommendation: Check board composition and how each seat is designated, which matters are reserved for enhanced approval, whether a deadlock-resolution mechanism exists, and how director removal or replacement works.
section_path: 1. BOARD COMPOSITION

1.1 Contracting Party A's board of directors shall consist of five (5) members: two (2) designated by the founders, one (1) designated by the Investor for so long as the Investor holds at least 5% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, and two (2) independent directors mutually agreed upon by the founders and the Investor.

recommendation.text_before_validation: Check board composition and how each seat is designated, which matters are reserved for enhanced approval, whether a deadlock-resolution mechanism exists, and how director removal or replacement works.

results.title: Financial Reporting Obligations
results._source_text_exact: 4.1 For so long as the Investor holds at least 3% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, Contracting Party A shall deliver to the Investor audited annual financial statements within ninety (90) days of fiscal year end, and unaudited quarterly financial statements within forty-five (45) days of each fiscal quarter end.
results.original_text: 4.1 For so long as the Investor holds at least 3% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, Contracting Party A shall deliver to the Investor audited annual financial statements within ninety (90) days of fiscal year end, and unaudited quarterly financial statements within forty-five (45) days of each fiscal quarter end.
results.reasoning_evidence: 4.1 For so long as the Investor holds at least 3% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, Contracting Party A shall deliver to the Investor audited annual financ
results.quoted_text: Contracting Party A shall deliver to the Investor audited annual financial statements within ninety (90) days of fiscal year end, and unaudited quarterly financial statements within forty-five (45) days of each fiscal quarter end.
results.explanation_simple: Contracting Party A is required to provide financial statements to the Investor based on their ownership stake.
results.why_it_matters: This clause ensures that the Investor receives timely financial information, which is crucial for monitoring their investment.
results.market_comparison: This clause is relatively standard in investment agreements, particularly for significant stakeholders.
results.recommendation: Check the delivery deadline for each report type, whether the reporting threshold (e.g. minimum ownership percentage) is reasonable, whether inspection/audit rights are included, and what remedy applies if delivery is late.
section_path: 4. INFORMATION RIGHTS

4.1 For so long as the Investor holds at least 3% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, Contracting Party A shall deliver to the Investor audited annual financial statements within ninety (90) days of fiscal year end, and unaudited quarterly financial statements within forty-five (45) days of each fiscal quarter end.

recommendation.text_before_validation: Check the delivery deadline for each report type, whether the reporting threshold (e.g. minimum ownership percentage) is reasonable, whether inspection/audit rights are included, and what remedy applies if delivery is late.

results.title: Investor Consent Rights
results._source_text_exact: 6.1 For so long as the Investor holds at least 5% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, Contracting Party A shall not, without the Investor's prior written consent: (a) amend its certificate of incorporation in a manner adverse to the Preferred Stock; (b) authorize a new class of stock senior to the Preferred Stock; or (c) incur indebtedness in excess of $2,000,000, other than in the ordinary course of business.
results.original_text: 6.1 For so long as the Investor holds at least 5% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, Contracting Party A shall not, without the Investor's prior written consent: (a) amend its certificate of incorporation in a manner adverse to the Preferred Stock; (b) authorize a new class of stock senior to the Preferred Stock; or (c) incur indebtedness in excess of $2,000,000, other than in the ordinary course of business.

results.quoted_text: Contracting Party A shall not, without the Investor's prior written consent: (a) amend its certificate of incorporation in a manner adverse to the Preferred Stock; (b) authorize a new class of stock senior to the Preferred Stock; or (c) incur indebtedness in excess of $2,000,000, other than in the ordinary course of business.
results.explanation_simple: The clause restricts Contracting Party A's actions regarding amendments to its incorporation, stock issuance, and incurring debt without the Investor's consent.
results.why_it_matters: This clause protects the Investor's interests by requiring consent for significant corporate actions that could affect their investment.
results.market_comparison: This type of clause is common in investment agreements, particularly for preferred stockholders, as it provides them with protective rights.

results.title: Termination
results._source_text_exact: 7.1 This Agreement shall terminate automatically upon the earlier of a Qualified IPO or a sale of Contracting Party A approved in accordance with Section 3.1.

results.legal_insight: This clause determines when the contract can end and whether the affected party has notice or an opportunity to cure.
results.recommendation: Review the termination triggers, notice requirements, cure periods, and post-termination obligations.

clauses._source_text_exact: 6.1 For so long as the Investor holds at least 5% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, Contracting Party A shall not, without the Investor's prior written consent: (a) amend its certificate of incorporation in a manner adverse to the Preferred Stock; (b) authorize a new class of stock senior to the Preferred Stock; or (c) incur indebtedness in excess of $2,000,000, other than in the ordinary course of business.
clauses.original_text: 6.1 For so long as the Investor holds at least 5% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, Contracting Party A shall not, without the Investor's prior written consent: (a) amend its certificate of incorporation in a manner adverse to the Preferred Stock; (b) authorize a new class of stock senior to the Preferred Stock; or (c) incur indebtedness in excess of $2,000,000, other than in the ordinary course of business.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 6.1 For so long as the Investor holds at least 5% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, Contracting Party A shall not, without the Investor's prior written con
clauses.quoted_text: Contracting Party A shall not, without the Investor's prior written consent: (a) amend its certificate of incorporation in a manner adverse to the Preferred Stock; (b) authorize a new class of stock senior to the Preferred Stock; or (c) incur indebtedness in excess of $2,000,000, other than in the ordinary course of business.
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause restricts Contracting Party A's actions regarding amendments to its incorporation, stock issuance, and incurring debt without the Investor's consent.
clauses.why_it_matters: This clause protects the Investor's interests by requiring consent for significant corporate actions that could affect their investment.
clauses.market_comparison: This type of clause is common in investment agreements, particularly for preferred stockholders, as it provides them with protective rights.
clauses.recommendation: Manual review required: generated text did not pass source-fidelity validation.
section_path: 6. PROTECTIVE PROVISIONS

6.1 For so long as the Investor holds at least 5% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, Contracting Party A shall not, without the Investor's prior written consent: (a) amend its certificate of incorporation in a manner adverse to the Preferred Stock; (b) authorize a new class of stock senior to the Preferred Stock; or (c) incur indebtedness in excess of $2,000,000, other than in the ordinary course of business.

clauses.title: Financial Reporting Obligations
clauses._source_text_exact: 4.1 For so long as the Investor holds at least 3% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, Contracting Party A shall deliver to the Investor audited annual financial statements within ninety (90) days of fiscal year end, and unaudited quarterly financial statements within forty-five (45) days of each fiscal quarter end.
clauses.original_text: 4.1 For so long as the Investor holds at least 3% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, Contracting Party A shall deliver to the Investor audited annual financial statements within ninety (90) days of fiscal year end, and unaudited quarterly financial statements within forty-five (45) days of each fiscal quarter end.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 4.1 For so long as the Investor holds at least 3% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, Contracting Party A shall deliver to the Investor audited annual financ
clauses.quoted_text: Contracting Party A shall deliver to the Investor audited annual financial statements within ninety (90) days of fiscal year end, and unaudited quarterly financial statements within forty-five (45) days of each fiscal quarter end.
clauses.explanation_simple: Contracting Party A is required to provide financial statements to the Investor based on their ownership stake.
clauses.why_it_matters: This clause ensures that the Investor receives timely financial information, which is crucial for monitoring their investment.
clauses.market_comparison: This clause is relatively standard in investment agreements, particularly for significant stakeholders.
clauses.recommendation: Check the delivery deadline for each report type, whether the reporting threshold (e.g. minimum ownership percentage) is reasonable, whether inspection/audit rights are included, and what remedy applies if delivery is late.
section_path: 4. INFORMATION RIGHTS

4.1 For so long as the Investor holds at least 3% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, Contracting Party A shall deliver to the Investor audited annual financial statements within ninety (90) days of fiscal year end, and unaudited quarterly financial statements within forty-five (45) days of each fiscal quarter end.

clauses.title: Termination
clauses._source_text_exact: 7.1 This Agreement shall terminate automatically upon the earlier of a Qualified IPO or a sale of Contracting Party A approved in accordance with Section 3.1.
clauses.original_text: 7.1 This Agreement shall terminate automatically upon the earlier of a Qualified IPO or a sale of Contracting Party A approved in accordance with Section 3.1.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 7.1 This Agreement shall terminate automatically upon the earlier of a Qualified IPO or a sale of Contracting Party A approved in accordance with Section 3.1.
clauses.quoted_text: This Agreement shall terminate automatically upon the earlier of a Qualified IPO or a sale of Contracting Party A approved in accordance with Section 3.1.
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause provides for automatic termination of the agreement under specific conditions.
clauses.why_it_matters: Automatic termination could lead to abrupt cessation of obligations and rights under the agreement, impacting business continuity.
clauses.legal_insight: This clause determines when the contract can end and whether the affected party has notice or an opportunity to cure.
clauses.recommendation: Review the termination triggers, notice requirements, cure periods, and post-termination obligations.
section_path: 7. TERMINATION
7.1 This Agreement shall terminate automatically upon the earlier of a Qualified IPO or a sale of Contracting Party A approved in accordance with Section 3.1.
risk_reasons: Termination provisions may affect operational continuity or legal remedies.

clauses.title: Drag-Along Right
clauses._source_text_exact: 3.1 If holders of a majority of the outstanding Preferred Stock and a majority of the board approve a sale of Contracting Party A, all other shareholders shall be required to vote in favor of and consent to such transaction ("Drag-Along Right"), subject to customary protections regarding consideration and representations.
clauses.original_text: 3.1 If holders of a majority of the outstanding Preferred Stock and a majority of the board approve a sale of Contracting Party A, all other shareholders shall be required to vote in favor of and consent to such transaction ("Drag-Along Right"), subject to customary protections regarding consideration and representations.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 3.1 If holders of a majority of the outstanding Preferred Stock and a majority of the board approve a sale of Contracting Party A, all other shareholders shall be requi
clauses.quoted_text: 3.1 If holders of a majority of the outstanding Preferred Stock and a majority of the board approve a sale of Contracting Party A, all other shareholders shall be required to vote in favor of and consent to such transaction ("Drag-Along Right"), subject to customary protections regarding consideration and r
clauses.explanation_simple: This clause mandates that minority shareholders must agree to a sale if a majority approves it.
clauses.why_it_matters: It can limit the ability of minority shareholders to oppose a sale, potentially impacting their financial interests.
clauses.legal_insight: This clause affects control, approvals, compliance duties, audit rights, assignment, or governance processes.
clauses.market_comparison: This clause is common in investment agreements to facilitate sales but may be seen as favoring majority shareholders.
clauses.red_flag_reason: The clause imposes significant restrictions on minority shareholders' rights, which could lead to potential conflicts.
clauses.recommendation: Check approval rights, assignment limits, audit scope, compliance obligations, subcontracting controls, and consequences of breach.
clauses.safer_alternative: Include provisions that ensure minority shareholders receive fair consideration and adequate representation during the sale process.
clauses.negotiation_advice: Consider requiring prior written consent for assignment, transfer or delegation, while allowing reasonable exceptions for affiliates, group restructuring, mergers, acquisitions, or sale of substantially all assets where the assignee can perform the obligations.
section_path: 3. DRAG-ALONG AND TAG-ALONG RIGHTS

3.1 If holders of a majority of the outstanding Preferred Stock and a majority of the board approve a sale of Contracting Party A, all other shareholders shall be required to vote in favor of and consent to such transaction ("Drag-Along Right"), subject to customary protections regarding consideration and representations.

clauses.title: Tag-Along Right
clauses._source_text_exact: 3.2 If a founder proposes to transfer shares representing more than 5% of Contracting Party A's outstanding capital stock, the Investor shall have the right to participate in such sale on the same terms ("Tag-Along Right").
clauses.original_text: 3.2 If a founder proposes to transfer shares representing more than 5% of Contracting Party A's outstanding capital stock, the Investor shall have the right to participate in such sale on the same terms ("Tag-Along Right").

clauses.quoted_text: 3.2 If a founder proposes to transfer shares representing more than 5% of Contracting Party A's outstanding capital stock, the Investor shall have the right to participate in such sale on the same terms ("Tag-Along Right").
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause grants the Investor a right to participate in share sales exceeding 5%.
clauses.why_it_matters: This right protects the Investor's interests by allowing them to sell shares under the same conditions as the founder, potentially preventing dilution of their investment.
clauses.legal_insight: This clause allocates ownership, use rights, licensing rights, or control over created or pre-existing intellectual property.
clauses.market_comparison: This clause is common in investment agreements to protect minority shareholders.

3.2 If a founder proposes to transfer shares representing more than 5% of Contracting Party A's outstanding capital stock, the Investor shall have the right to participate in such sale on the same terms ("Tag-Along Right").

clauses.title: Investor's Right to Purchase
clauses._source_text_exact: 2.1 The Investor shall have the right to purchase its pro rata share of any new securities issued by Contracting Party A, subject to customary exceptions for equity incentive plan issuances, acquisitions, and Qualified IPO issuances.
clauses.original_text: 2.1 The Investor shall have the right to purchase its pro rata share of any new securities issued by Contracting Party A, subject to customary exceptions for equity incentive plan issuances, acquisitions, and Qualified IPO issuances.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 2.1 The Investor shall have the right to purchase its pro rata share of any new securities issued by Contracting Party A, subject to customary exceptions for equity inc
clauses.quoted_text: The Investor shall have the right to purchase its pro rata share of any new securities issued by Contracting Party A, subject to customary exceptions for equity incentive plan issuances, acquisitions, and Qualified IPO issuances.
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause grants the Investor a right to purchase new securities, which could affect ownership stakes.
clauses.why_it_matters: This right allows the Investor to maintain their proportional ownership in Contracting Party A, which is significant during new issuances of securities.
clauses.legal_insight: This clause allocates ownership, use rights, licensing rights, or control over created or pre-existing intellectual property.
clauses.market_comparison: This clause is common in investment agreements, particularly for venture capital and private equity transactions.
clauses.recommendation: Confirm ownership of pre-existing materials, newly created deliverables, license scope, usage restrictions, moral rights, and post-termination rights.
clauses.negotiation_advice: Consider clarifying ownership, permitted use, licensing scope, background IP, newly created deliverables, improvements, derivative works, approval rights, moral rights, transfer timing, and post-termination restrictions.
section_path: 2. PREEMPTIVE RIGHTS

2.1 The Investor shall have the right to purchase its pro rata share of any new securities issued by Contracting Party A, subject to customary exceptions for equity incentive plan issuances, acquisitions, and Qualified IPO issuances.

clauses.title: Shareholder Transfer Restrictions
clauses._source_text_exact: 5.1 No shareholder shall transfer any shares of Contracting Party A's capital stock except in compliance with this Agreement, including a right of first refusal in favor of Contracting Party A and then the other shareholders, and compliance with applicable securities laws.
clauses.original_text: 5.1 No shareholder shall transfer any shares of Contracting Party A's capital stock except in compliance with this Agreement, including a right of first refusal in favor of Contracting Party A and then the other shareholders, and compliance with applicable securities laws.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 5.1 No shareholder shall transfer any shares of Contracting Party A's capital stock except in compliance with this Agreement, including a right of first refusal in favo
clauses.quoted_text: No shareholder shall transfer any shares of Contracting Party A's capital stock except in compliance with this Agreement, including a right of first refusal in favor of Contracting Party A and then the other shareholders, and compliance with applicable securities laws.
clauses.explanation_simple: This clause restricts the transfer of shares by shareholders.
clauses.why_it_matters: It ensures that any transfer of shares is controlled and complies with the agreement and legal requirements, which can affect shareholder liquidity and control.
clauses.legal_insight: This clause affects control, approvals, compliance duties, audit rights, assignment, or governance processes.
clauses.market_comparison: This type of clause is common in shareholder agreements to maintain control within the existing shareholder group.
clauses.recommendation: Check approval rights, assignment limits, audit scope, compliance obligations, subcontracting controls, and consequences of breach.
clauses.negotiation_advice: Consider requiring prior written consent for assignment, transfer or delegation, while allowing reasonable exceptions for affiliates, group restructuring, mergers, acquisitions, or sale of substantially all assets where the assignee can perform the obligations.
section_path: 5. TRANSFER RESTRICTIONS

5.1 No shareholder shall transfer any shares of Contracting Party A's capital stock except in compliance with this Agreement, including a right of first refusal in favor of Contracting Party A and then the other shareholders, and compliance with applicable securities laws.
risk_reasons: Compliance provisions may create ongoing legal, regulatory, or policy obligations.

clauses.title: Board of Directors Composition
clauses._source_text_exact: 1.1 Contracting Party A's board of directors shall consist of five (5) members: two (2) designated by the founders, one (1) designated by the Investor for so long as the Investor holds at least 5% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, and two (2) independent directors mutually agreed upon by the founders and the Investor.
clauses.original_text: 1.1 Contracting Party A's board of directors shall consist of five (5) members: two (2) designated by the founders, one (1) designated by the Investor for so long as the Investor holds at least 5% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, and two (2) independent directors mutually agreed upon by the founders and the Investor.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 1.1 Contracting Party A's board of directors shall consist of five (5) members: two (2) designated by the founders, one (1) designated by the Investor for so long as th
clauses.quoted_text: Contracting Party A's board of directors shall consist of five (5) members: two (2) designated by the founders, one (1) designated by the Investor for so long as the Investor holds at least 5% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, and two (2) independent directors mutually agreed upon by the founders and the Investor.
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause specifies the composition of the board of directors, including designated members by founders and the Investor.
clauses.why_it_matters: The composition of the board affects decision-making power and control within Contracting Party A, particularly the influence of the Investor.
clauses.market_comparison: This structure is somewhat common in startup governance, where founders retain significant control while allowing for investor representation.
clauses.recommendation: Check board composition and how each seat is designated, which matters are reserved for enhanced approval, whether a deadlock-resolution mechanism exists, and how director removal or replacement works.
section_path: 1. BOARD COMPOSITION

1.1 Contracting Party A's board of directors shall consist of five (5) members: two (2) designated by the founders, one (1) designated by the Investor for so long as the Investor holds at least 5% of Contracting Party A's fully-diluted capitalization, and two (2) independent directors mutually agreed upon by the founders and the Investor.
